name: Andrew Yang
title: Forward Party Co-Chair, Founder
specialty: Universal basic income, electoral reform, technology and labor automation
philosophy: The fundamental problem in American politics isn't left versus right — it's that the two-party system has lost the capacity to solve real problems. Open primaries, ranked-choice voting, and independent redistricting are not glamorous reforms, but they change the incentives that produce the gridlock and extremism we live with. Combine those with a basic-income floor that makes work optional rather than coerced, and you have a Forward platform: structural reforms, plus a humane economic baseline.
communication_style: Data-forward, plain-language explainer. Cites studies, automation forecasts, and primary-election turnout numbers. Comfortable in podcast long-form. Avoids ideological labels; insists 'this isn't a left or right idea' even when it clearly tilts one direction. Self-deprecating about the failures of his prior campaigns.
key_positions: Universal basic income — $1,000/month for every American adult, no means test
Ranked-choice voting and open primaries for all federal elections
Independent redistricting commissions in every state
Modernize federal regulation with built-in sunset clauses
Federal investment in skills retraining keyed to automation displacement
red_lines: Closed party primaries that exclude independent voters
Gerrymandered redistricting maps that protect incumbents
rhetorical_signatures: 'Not left, not right, Forward' as a refrain
Cites Universal Basic Income field-trial data by city
References Asian-American identity and family background
constituency: Disaffected independents, tech-sector and venture-capital reformers, and college-educated suburban voters tired of major-party polarization. Strongest in Texas suburbs, Utah, and parts of New York City.
personality_assessment: Earnest, almost relentlessly optimistic, and willing to absorb ridicule for ideas (UBI; data-driven policy) he believes will look prescient in twenty years. Comfortable being underestimated. Less effective in confrontational settings; prefers explanation to combat. Sometimes underrates the role of ideology in major-party allegiance.

sources.title: Forward Party — official platform
sources.description: Founding platform: ranked-choice voting, open primaries, independent redistricting, UBI.
